display_name: LAG creation and member add to ASIC
trigger: 'config portchannel add PortChannel01' followed by 'config portchannel member add PortChannel01 EthernetX'

steps.action: User runs 'config portchannel add PortChannel01'. add_portchannel() validates the name and writes an entry to CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL table with fields admin_status=up, mtu=9100, lacp_key=auto, fast_rate.

TeamMgr::doTask() dispatches CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL set-event to doLagTask(). doLagTask() calls addLag() which spawns 'teamd -r -t PortChannel01 -c <json-config> -d' with a generated LACP JSON config containing device name, MAC, runner=lacp. The kernel creates a team netdevice for PortChannel01.
steps.output: teamd process running for PortChannel01; team net device visible in kernel
steps.verify: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel01 state view

steps.action: After addLag() returns task_success, doLagTask() calls setLagMtu() which issues 'ip link set dev PortChannel01 mtu 9100' and writes mtu field to APPL_DB LAG_TABLE via m_appLagTable.set(). Also calls setLagAdminStatus() to bring the interface up.

TeamSync::onMsg() receives RTM_NEWLINK netlink event for PortChannel01 (type=team). Calls TeamSync::addLag() which writes admin_status, oper_status, mtu fields to APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and initialises a TeamPortSync instance that connects to teamd via libteam to listen for port-change events. STATE_DB LAG_TABLE is also updated with state=ok.

User runs 'config portchannel member add PortChannel01 EthernetX'. add_portchannel_member() validates that the port exists, is not already in a portchannel, has matching speed/MTU, and writes CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER entry.

TeamMgr::doLagMemberTask() fires on CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER set-event. Waits for port state and LAG state to be ok (isPortStateOk/isLagStateOk), then calls addLagMember() which runs: (1) 'ip link set dev EthernetX down', (2) 'teamdctl PortChannel01 port config update EthernetX {lacp_key:N,link_watch:{name:ethtool}}', (3) 'teamdctl PortChannel01 port add EthernetX'. The port is now enslaved into the team device and LACP PDU exchange begins.
steps.output: EthernetX enslaved into PortChannel01 team device; LACP negotiation started
steps.verify: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel01 state view | grep EthernetX

steps.action: TeamSync::TeamPortSync::onChange() fires via libteam port-change callback (registered via team_change_handler_register). Iterates over team ports using team_for_each_port, maps ifindex to ifname, reads enabled status from team_get_port_enabled(). Writes APPL_DB LAG_MEMBER_TABLE entry 'PortChannel01:EthernetX' with status=enabled (after LACP negotiation succeeds) or status=disabled (while negotiating).

PortsOrch::doLagTask() is notified of APPL_DB LAG_TABLE change via ConsumerStateTable. Calls PortsOrch::addLag() which invokes sai_lag_api->create_lag() to create a SAI LAG object. The resulting sai_object_id is stored in ASIC_DB under SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG.

PortsOrch::doLagMemberTask() is notified of APPL_DB LAG_MEMBER_TABLE change. After confirming the LAG and port objects exist, calls PortsOrch::addLagMember() which sets SAI_LAG_MEMBER_ATTR_LAG_ID and SAI_LAG_MEMBER_ATTR_PORT_ID, and optionally SAI_LAG_MEMBER_ATTR_INGRESS_DISABLE/EGRESS_DISABLE (for disabled members) then invokes sai_lag_api->create_lag_member(). The resulting sai_object_id is stored in ASIC_DB under SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER.

Syncd::processSingleEvent() reads CREATE commands from ASIC_DB for SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG and SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER. Routes through processQuadEvent() with SAI_COMMON_API_CREATE. Translates virtual-to-real OIDs via the translator, then calls the vendor SAI library to program the LAG and LAG member in ASIC hardware.
steps.output: LAG and LAG member programmed in hardware; traffic hashing across member ports begins after LACP converges
steps.verify: show interfaces portchannel (should show PortChannel01 with member EthernetX)

failure_injection_points.failure: teammgrd not processing CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL event
failure_injection_points.symptom: CONFIG_DB has PORTCHANNEL entry but no teamd process running and no LAG_TABLE in APPL_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec teamd supervisorctl status teammgrd; check /var/log/swss/teammgrd.log; verify CONFIG_DB subscription: redis-cli -n 4 SUBSCRIBE '__keyevent@4__:hset'

failure_injection_points.symptom: teammgrd retries addLag; no 'teamd' process visible for PortChannel01; LAG_TABLE not in APPL_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec teamd ps aux | grep teamd; check /var/log/swss/teammgrd.log for 'Failed to start port channel'; verify teamd binary: docker exec teamd which teamd

failure_injection_points.failure: teamsyncd not receiving RTM_NEWLINK for team device
failure_injection_points.symptom: LAG_TABLE in APPL_DB has only mtu (from teammgrd) but missing admin_status/oper_status fields; STATE_DB LAG_TABLE has no entry
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec teamd supervisorctl status teamsyncd; check /var/log/swss/teamsyncd.log; verify netlink: ip link show type team

failure_injection_points.failure: teammgrd waiting for port or LAG state before enslaving member
failure_injection_points.symptom: CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER exists but EthernetX is not enslaved; no LAG_MEMBER_TABLE entry in APPL_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: redis-cli -n 6 HGETALL 'PORT_TABLE|EthernetX' (check state=ok); redis-cli -n 6 HGETALL 'LAG_TABLE|PortChannel01' (check state=ok); check /var/log/swss/teammgrd.log for retry messages

failure_injection_points.failure: LACP not converging; teamsyncd writes status=disabled
failure_injection_points.symptom: LAG_MEMBER_TABLE:PortChannel01:EthernetX status=disabled; portchannel not forwarding traffic; peer side may show LACP timeout
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec teamd teamdctl PortChannel01 state view; check LACP PDU exchange: tcpdump -i EthernetX ether proto 0x8809; verify LACP timers and system priority match on both sides

failure_injection_points.symptom: LAG_TABLE in APPL_DB but no SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG entry in ASIC_DB
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec swss supervisorctl status orchagent; check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for SAI errors; redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG:*'

failure_injection_points.failure: orchagent addLagMember fails because LAG SAI object not yet created when member event arrives
failure_injection_points.symptom: LAG_MEMBER_TABLE in APPL_DB but no SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER in ASIC_DB; orchagent log shows 'Failed to locate LAG'
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: This is a transient ordering issue; orchagent will retry. check /var/log/swss/orchagent.log for 'Failed to locate LAG PortChannel01'; verify: redis-cli -n 1 KEYS 'ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG:*'

failure_injection_points.failure: syncd SAI call fails for LAG or LAG_MEMBER creation
failure_injection_points.symptom: ASIC_DB has SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG or SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_LAG_MEMBER entry but syncd reports SAI_STATUS_FAILURE; portchannel not operational in hardware
failure_injection_points.diagnosis: docker exec syncd supervisorctl status syncd; check /var/log/syncd.log for SAI error codes; check if LAG resource limits exceeded: show system resources
